title: Proximal Tubule Bicarbonate Reabsorption
buzzwords: reclaims most filtered base, no net acid change
CO2 shuttle across the apical membrane
sodium-coupled antiporter pumps H+ into lumen
basolateral cotransporter exports reclaimed anion
handles 70-80% of the daily filtered load
stimulated by angiotensin II and volume depletion
NHE3 apical, NBCe1 basolateral
blocked by acetazolamide via carbonic anhydrase inhibition

title: Alpha-Intercalated Cell H+ Secretion
buzzwords: distal segment sets the final urine pH
secretes acid and regenerates new base
limited by ~1000-fold gradient, urine floor 4.6
upregulated by systemic acidosis
potassium-sparing exchanger contributes to secretion
stimulated by aldosterone in collecting duct
H+-ATPase and H+/K+-ATPase on apical membrane
hyperaldosteronism triad: HTN, hypokalemia, alkalosis

title: Beta-Intercalated Cell Bicarbonate Secretion
buzzwords: alkalinizes urine up to pH 8.0
activated during systemic alkalemia
mirror image of its acid-secreting sibling
exchanges luminal chloride for intracellular base
helps offload excess filtered HCO3- load
collecting duct cell type, not proximal
apical Cl-/HCO3- exchanger
pendrin-mediated bicarbonate secretion

title: Titratable Acid Buffering (Phosphate)
buzzwords: filtered weak acids sponge secreted H+
covers about half of daily acid excretion
not specifically regulated, depends on filtration
lost capacity contributes to acidosis of CKD
includes citrate and sulfate as minor players
cannot ramp up during acid load
H2PO4-/HPO4(2-) buffer system in tubule
phosphate is the dominant filtered buffer (~50%)

title: Ammoniagenesis and NH4+ Trapping
buzzwords: the kidney's most adjustable acid-excretion route
can ramp ~5-fold during systemic acidosis
generates one new base molecule per cycle
suppressed by hyperkalemia, enhanced by hypokalemia
lets urine excrete acid beyond the pH 4.6 floor
impaired ammoniagenesis drives NAGMA of CKD
glutamine metabolism in PCT yields NH4+ and HCO3-
type 4 RTA: low aldosterone blocks NH4+ production
